title: Detecting disclosure of generative-AI use
text: Since 2023, journals publishers (ICMJE, COPE, Nature, Science, Elsevier others) asked authors disclose use generative AI ChatGPT, GPT-4, Copilot, Gemini large language models preparing manuscript. rt_ai_pmc() detects whether article carries disclosure. deliberately narrow. targets AI used prepare manuscript (writing, editing, language, figures), counts directions disclosure: positive disclosure: “authors used ChatGPT improve readability manuscript”; negative disclosure: “generative AI used preparation work”; dedicated section: “Declaration generative AI” heading. count AI article’s research method (example paper trains deep-learning classifier, one studies ChatGPT subject). mention AI heavily make statement using AI write paper, disclosures. negative lookahead also keeps tool sense “large language model” read something authors edited.

title: Detecting disclosure of generative-AI use
text: practice exist 2023, evaluating older articles meaningless. rt_ai_pmc() therefore reads publication year : returns is_ai_pred = NA articles 2023 (reports year); returns TRUE FALSE 2023 onward. bundled example article 2020, returns NA: three possible values easy act : TRUE (disclosure found), FALSE (article 2023 later carries disclosure), NA (article predates practice assessed). rt_summary() drops NAs, corpus prevalence computed articles indicator applies.
code: xml_path <- system.file(   "extdata", "PMID32171256-PMC7071725.xml", package = "rtransparency" ) ai <- rt_ai_pmc(xml_path, remove_ns = TRUE) c(year = ai$year, is_ai_pred = ai$is_ai_pred) #>       year is_ai_pred  #>       2020         NA

title: Introduction to rtransparency
text: rtransparency identifies extracts indicators transparency full text published biomedical articles. works two inputs: plain TXT files (typically converted PDFs) PMC XML files (JATS XML served PubMed Central). indicator returns whether indicator found , found, sentence statement triggered detection. rt_all_pmc runs ten detectors together single pass: COI, funding, registration, novelty, replication, data sharing, code sharing, AI-use disclosure, open-access licensing reporting-guideline use. (rt_all covers first five TXT; others also standalone TXT detectors, rt_data_code, rt_ai, rt_oa rt_reporting, part rt_all wrapper.) AI-use disclosure newest indicator. Journals asked authors disclose use generative AI (ChatGPT similar) preparing manuscript since 2023, rt_ai_pmc evaluates indicator articles published 2023 later returns NA earlier ones. package validation described Serghiou et al., Assessment transparency indicators across biomedical literature: open open? (PLOS Biology, 2021, doi:10.1371/journal.pbio.3001107).

title: Introduction to rtransparency
text: PMC XML parsed xml2. XML root standardized <article> node (package accepts OAI-PMH, EFetch <pmc-articleset> bare <article> shapes), namespace optionally stripped (remove_ns = TRUE), text split sections indicator usually appears: acknowledgments, footnotes / author notes, body, methods, abstract supplementary material. TXT files read whole split paragraphs.

title: Introduction to rtransparency
text: Detection rule-based interpretable: indicator curated set regular expressions applied relevant sections, rather machine learning model. keeps output auditable (matched statement returned) reproducible. Conflicts interest. Detected structured COI footnotes (fn-type = "conflict"), section titles (“Conflicts interest”, “Competing interests”, “Declaration interest”, “Duality interest”), set text patterns covering financial relationships, consulting, fees, board membership, patents explicit “competing interests” declarations. Honoraria--subjects reference text masked reduce false positives. Funding. Detected XML <funding-group> element, funding section titles, text patterns “supported ”, “funded ”, “grant / number”, named funders award types. Acknowledged funding required use explicit funding language (funding verb tied funder), bare mention institution word “support” enough. -funding declarations excluded. Protocol registration. Detected registry identifiers (ClinicalTrials.gov NCT, PROSPERO CRD, ISRCTN, ANZCTR ACTRN, DRKS, IRCT, UMIN, ChiCTR) registration phrasing methods footnotes. Novelty replication. Detected claim patterns “first time”, “knowledge”, “novel finding” (novelty) “replicate”, “independently validated”, “confirmatory cohort” (replication), negation filters (“failed replicate”). Data code sharing. Detected native detector (.detect_data_code) built public repository facts curated benchmark statements: field-specific accession schemes (GEO GSE, SRA / BioProject PRJNA, PDB, ArrayExpress, dbGaP, ProteomeXchange, Dryad / Zenodo / figshare DOIs, …), repository URLs names, deposit / availability / data-availability-statement language, supplement file-format signals. Crucially distinguishes sharing (“data deposited GEO”) reuse (“data downloaded GEO”) excludes “available request”. Code repositories (GitHub, GitLab, Bitbucket) count data paired data noun, code-GitHub link mistaken data sharing. AI-use disclosure. Detected “Declaration generative AI” type section title, text names generative-AI tool (ChatGPT, GPT-4, Copilot, Gemini, LLM, …) manuscript-preparation context (“used ChatGPT improve readability”) explicit negation (“generative AI used”). negative lookahead keeps tool sense “large language model” writing-object pattern, AI used purely research method (writing) counted. evaluated 2023 onward. Open-access licensing. Read JATS <license> element license-reference URL, classified canonical identifier (CC--4.0, CC--NC-ND-4.0, CC0-1.0, …). Creative Commons CC0 license (explicit open-access declaration) sets is_open_access; CC0 data-waiver mistaken article license. reuse (“R”) dimension FAIR feeds rfair assessment. Reporting-guideline use. Detected authors state followed reporting guideline (EQUATOR checklists: CONSORT, PRISMA extensions, STROBE, ARRIVE, STARD, TRIPOD, COREQ, SQUIRE, CHEERS, CARE, wider reportilo list), returning one. Detection precision-first: guideline counts reporting context; common-word acronyms (ARRIVE, CARE, RECORD, …) require upper-case form beside guideline noun; animal-welfare (“Care Use Laboratory Animals”), clinical-practice non-adherence mentions excluded.

title: Scope and limitations
text: Language. Detection strongest English. Conflict--interest funding statements also detected Spanish, Portuguese, French, German Italian; indicators languages yet covered. Data availability “upon request”. Data offered request counted shared, reflecting modern open-data standard. stricter earlier definitions report lower data-sharing prevalence tools count availability--request. Novelty replication claim detection. identify authors state, whether study truly novel replication succeeded. replication indicator particular precision-limited validation language (“validation cohort”, “independent”) heavily overloaded; see replication-enriched benchmark inst/benchmark/. Plain text vs XML. plain-text detectors share logic PMC XML detectors use XML-structural cues (tagged funding groups, conflict footnotes, section types), statements detectable XML detectable plain text. plain-text AI detector rt_ai() special case: publication date section structure available, applies 2023 year gate (never returns NA) scans whole document, caller must restrict 2023--later articles tolerate higher false-positive rate AI-method papers rt_ai_pmc(). Reporting guideline claim detection. identifies stated adherence reporting guideline, whether study fully complied. tuned precision (validated 93.8% sensitivity / 99.0% specificity hand-labeled 1000-article sample); residual misses mostly non-English statements, residual false positives guidelines named actually followed article. Open-access licensing XML-structural. reads JATS <license> element, near-deterministic PMC XML , plain text, depends license statement present extracted text. Accuracy correction. rt_summary() can correct apparent prevalence using bundled sensitivity/specificity estimates (rt_accuracy). derive validation benchmarks; supply via rt_summary(accuracy = ) study-specific estimates. AI disclosure reported uncorrected (prevalence low unselected literature stable estimate).

code: s <- rt_summary(rt_demo) knitr::kable(   s[, c("label", "n_articles", "n_detected", "percent", "conf_low", "conf_high")],   digits = 1,   col.names = c("Indicator", "Assessed", "Detected", "%", "CI low", "CI high") )

title: Summarizing transparency across a corpus
text: text-mining detector perfect, observed prevalence biased estimate true prevalence. rt_summary() corrects using detector’s sensitivity specificity estimates (Rogan-Gladen estimator). correction default adds adj_percent, adj_low adj_high: accuracy values come rt_accuracy: AI-use disclosure bundled accuracy estimate , corrected value NA. Novelty’s estimate comes hand-labeled gold set (inst/benchmark/results_novelty_replication.md); data/code values reproducible benchmark estimates native detector, untouched external-validation estimates. Replication’s correction approximate: sensitivity comes replication-enriched sample specificity representative 2023 sample, rest single-design validation conflicts interest, funding registration, Rogan-Gladen interval propagate uncertainty estimates. use validation (published oddpub values data code), pass table variable, sensitivity specificity columns:
code: knitr::kable(   s[, c("label", "percent", "adj_percent", "adj_low", "adj_high")],   digits = 1,   col.names = c("Indicator", "Apparent %", "Corrected %", "CI low", "CI high") ) rt_accuracy #> # A tibble: 8 × 5 #>   variable            label                 sensitivity specificity source       #>   <chr>               <chr>                       <dbl>       <dbl> <chr>        #> 1 is_coi_pred         Conflicts of interest       0.992       0.995 Serghiou et… #> 2 is_fund_pred        Funding disclosure          0.997       0.981 Serghiou et… #> 3 is_register_pred    Protocol registration       0.955       0.997 Serghiou et… #> 4 is_open_data        Data sharing                0.765       0.99  rtransparen… #> 5 is_open_code        Code sharing                0.881       0.995 rtransparen… #> 6 is_novelty_pred     Novelty                     0.838       0.952 rtransparen… #> 7 is_replication_pred Replication                 0.928       0.985 rtransparen… #> 8 is_reporting_pred   Reporting guideline         0.938       0.99  rtransparen… my_acc <- rt_accuracy my_acc$sensitivity[my_acc$variable == "is_open_data"] <- 0.758 rt_summary(rt_demo, indicators = "is_open_data", accuracy = my_acc)[,   c("label", "percent", "adj_percent")] #> # A tibble: 1 × 3 #>   label        percent adj_percent #>   <chr>          <dbl>       <dbl> #> 1 Data sharing    20.4        26.0

what: Identify disclosure of generative-AI use from a TXT file. — rt_ai
title: Identify disclosure of generative-AI use from a TXT file. — rt_ai
text: Detects whether article discloses use (non-use) generative AI AI-assisted tools preparing manuscript, plain-text (typically PDF-derived) file. Unlike [rt_ai_pmc()] applies **publication-year gate**: plain-text file carries reliable publication date, `is_ai_pred` always `TRUE` `FALSE` (never `NA`). AI-use disclosure became expected practice 2023, caller responsible restricting analysis articles 2023 onward. Plain text also lacks section structure PMC detector uses confine scan back matter, acknowledgments declaration sections, article uses AI purely research method likely flagged [rt_ai_pmc()].

what: Summarize transparency indicators across a corpus of articles — rt_summary
title: Summarize transparency indicators across a corpus of articles — rt_summary
text: Takes data frame one row per article (output [rt_all_pmc()] joined [rt_data_code_pmc()], stacked many articles) returns prevalence transparency indicator. indicator reports number articles assessed, number indicator detected, apparent prevalence Wilson confidence interval , optionally, prevalence corrected detector's sensitivity specificity (Rogan-Gladen estimator).

code: rt_summary(   data,   indicators = NULL,   by = NULL,   adjust = TRUE,   accuracy = NULL,   conf_level = 0.95 )

title: Summarize transparency indicators across a corpus of articles — rt_summary
text: data data frame one row per article. Indicator columns must logical numeric 0/1 named [rt_all_pmc()]: `is_coi_pred`, `is_fund_pred`, `is_register_pred`, `is_open_data`, `is_open_code`, `is_novelty_pred`, `is_replication_pred`, `is_ai_pred`, `is_open_access` `is_reporting_pred`. `NA` marks article assessed indicator (example `is_ai_pred` 2023) excluded denominator. values rejected rather silently coerced. indicators Optional character vector indicator columns summarize. Defaults every recognized indicator present `data`. Optional name grouping column (example publication year, journal article type); summary computed within group. adjust `TRUE` (default), add prevalence corrected detector sensitivity specificity using `accuracy`. Indicators absent `accuracy` receive `NA` corrected values. accuracy data frame detector accuracy columns `variable`, `sensitivity` `specificity`. Defaults [rt_accuracy]. conf_level Confidence level intervals (default `0.95`).

title: Summarize transparency indicators across a corpus of articles — rt_summary

title: rtransparency 1.1.0
text: Two new transparency indicators, bringing total ten. Open-access licensing (rt_oa_pmc(), rt_oa(), within rt_all_pmc()): detects whether article openly licensed canonical license (is_open_access, oa_license; example CC--4.0, CC--NC-ND-4.0, CC0-1.0), reuse (“R”) dimension FAIR. Read JATS <license> element. Validated 100% sensitivity / 100% specificity, 99.8% license-type accuracy, 1000-article 2023 sample. Reporting-guideline use (rt_reporting_pmc(), rt_reporting(), within rt_all_pmc()): detects whether authors state followed reporting guideline one (is_reporting_pred, reporting_guideline), covering EQUATOR catalogue (CONSORT, PRISMA extensions, STROBE, ARRIVE, STARD, TRIPOD, COREQ, SRQR, SQUIRE, CHEERS, CARE, PROCESS, STROCSS, … wider reportilo guideline list). Precision-first: animal-welfare, clinical non-adherence mentions excluded. Validated 93.8% sensitivity / 99.0% specificity (65 positives) 1000-article 2023 sample, every article hand-labeled maintainer. rt_all_pmc() now returns ten indicators; rt_summary() bundled rt_accuracy table cover two new ones.

New rt_ai(), plain-text detector generative-AI-use disclosure, text counterpart rt_ai_pmc(). text file carries reliable publication date, rt_ai() applies 2023 year gate (is_ai_pred always TRUE/FALSE, never NA) confine scan back-matter sections; restrict articles 2023 onward. text: release overhauls novelty detector recall precision, fixes two long-standing bugs public PMC entry points, corrects mislabeled articles 2023 validation sample. Novelty recall. Data sharing recall improved. detector now recognizes “data supporting findings included within article / within manuscript” availability statements (previously “article” wording matched). held-benchmark Serghiou et al. (2021), data sensitivity rises 72.2% 76.5% specificity unchanged 99.0% (PPV 98.9%); rt_accuracy updated. Replication precision improved. Future required validation framed -yet-done (“external validation essential/needed clinical implementation”, “finding requires validation independent cohorts”) longer counted replication. Validation actually performed (external independent) still counts. change gated modal need word performed validation preserved. Added regression tests changes.

title: rtransparent 0.6.1
text: Precision recall fixes independent manual review sample open-access PMC articles: Replication: bare internal training/validation split (single dataset divided training cohort validation cohort) longer counted replication, since model development rather independent confirmation new sample. External independent validation still counts. Novelty: firstness claims carry adverbial now detected, example “first date report …” “first ever study characterize …”. Data: generic publisher line “online version contains supplementary material available ”, appears every article journals, longer treated data-availability statement .

title: rtransparent 0.6.0
text: Novelty replication detection now scan full article body recognize many phrasings (firstness knowledge-gap claims novelty; internal, external independent-validation claims replication), new suppressors keep generic “validation” future-work wording . Data code sharing detection gained high-specificity patterns -article supplement availability statements, repository-hosted data, explicit code-availability wording, new vetoes supplement boilerplate, result tables, local file paths tool/package-use mentions. Registration detection added negation guards (“registered”, “applicable”, IRB-numbers) broader registry coverage. reproducible data/code benchmark now gives data 71.3% sensitivity / 99.0% specificity code 83.5% sensitivity / 99.5% specificity; rt_accuracy updated estimates. Internal helper functions now marked @noRd, manual pkgdown reference present public API. Hardened rt_summary() rt_score() indicator columns must logical numeric 0/1 values, NA allowed. Added reproducible external validation harness data-raw/external-validation/.

title: rtransparent 0.3.3
text: Improved funding specificity 78% 96% held-XML test set (accuracy 86% 97%) tightening get_fund_acknow_new(). previously flagged acknowledgment merely named institution used word “support”, competing-interest statements, generic thanks, data-availability statements affiliations misread funding. now requires explicit funding language: funding verb directed funder, institutional “support/funding …”, grant award identifier, named award. Sensitivity unchanged 100% test set.
